title: Pouzolzia boiviniana
taxonomy: Pouzolzia boiviniana is classified under the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This family includes a wide range of plants, from stinging nettles to various species of vines and shrubs. Pouzolzia boiviniana is a type of flowering plant, with its scientific classification including the kingdom Plantae, followed by Magnoliophyta for its flowering nature, and then the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it in the order Rosales, which includes plants with a combination of nettle-like and rose-like characteristics. At the most specific level, Pouzolzia boiviniana is classified under the family Urticaceae as Urticaceae, Pouzolzia, Pouzolzia boiviniana.
uses: Pouzolzia boiviniana is a plant species native to Madagascar, and it has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. The leaves and stems of the plant are used to treat different ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Pouzolzia boiviniana is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its astringent properties.
